finding.title: Silent write failure on HTML output
finding.body: except OSError: pass swallows all write failures. If the HTML file cannot be written (disk full, permissions, read-only directory), the hook exits 0 on line 232 with zero indication of failure. The caller has no way to know the companion HTML was not generated.
finding.fix_constraint: Log the failure to stderr before passing; the hook must not block the tool (PostToolUse).

finding.title: Dead code: _write_md_and_run never called
finding.body: The helper _write_md_and_run is defined at module scope but never invoked by any test in the file. All tests call _run_hook directly instead. Dead code adds maintenance burden with zero value.
finding.fix_constraint: Remove the unused function. Do not add tests that call it — the existing pattern (_run_hook directly) is sufficient.

finding.title: Title inserted into HTML without escaping
finding.body: _extract_title returns raw text from the first # heading in the markdown. This raw text is inserted via .format() into the HTML title tag and h1 element without any HTML-escaping. If a heading contains angle brackets, ampersands, or quotes, they will appear as literal HTML control characters in the output. While this is local file generation (not served), well-formed HTML should escape text content.
finding.fix_constraint: Apply html.escape() to the title before inserting into the template. The body (from _md_to_html) is already converted, but title bypasses _inline_format.

verified_clean.category: main() signature (-> None) checked; hook reads JSON from stdin and writes HTML — contract matches PostToolUse hook spec. All function signatures have complete type hints.
No selectors, queries, or engine integrations in changed code.
All file opens use with-blocks (lines 213, 227). stdin read via json.load() — single-use, no handle leak. No subprocess or socket resources.
All variables defined before use. No unbound references.
No numeric indexing or boundary arithmetic in changed code. String slicing uses fixed offsets safe against empty strings (preceded by startswith checks).
Hook runs as a separate process per PostToolUse invocation. No shared mutable state, no threads, no async.
CSS values in _html_template are design constants. The .md/.html extension strings and ("Write", "Edit") tuple are structural to the hook's purpose. No actionable magic values.
No parallel sites identified. The .claude/ exemption pattern matches the one in #412 — consistent.
